display.usage: Headlines, hero text, event names, pull quotes, poster copy. The personality font. Big Shoulders Display is ultra-condensed and upright with the same commanding stature as Monument Extended — making it feel like the Run Club shares a design bloodline with the main Mozaka brand. Where Monument is industrial-night, Big Shoulders is athletic-dawn: the same authority, more kinetic.

body.usage: Body copy, captions, UI labels, WhatsApp blasts, onboarding copy, social captions. Warm, geometric, and highly legible at small sizes on a phone screen. The voice Mozaka uses when talking to you, not at you.

mono.usage: Times, paces, distances, run stats, split data, event dates. Makes '5:32/km' and '7:00AM' feel like a dashboard readout — technical, earned, cool. Never use for prose.

rules: Syne 800 is the shout — one line, hero use only. Never wrap it, never set it smaller than 48px, never use it for body copy.
All Syne display text should be uppercase or title case. Sentence case kills the geometry. The letterforms are built to stand tall.
DM Sans 300 is ambient — long descriptions, story copy, the runners' bible. 400 is default UI. 500 is inline emphasis. 700 is buttons and labels only.
Space Mono appears only when the content is a number or time-based fact: 5km, 5:00/km, 7:00AM, 12 runners, Sat 18 Jan. If it's a sentence, it's not mono.
Minimum body font size: 16px mobile, 18px desktop. Mozaka copy is read on phones after a run. Squinting is a failure state.
Never mix Syne and Space Mono in the same text block. They serve different registers — voice vs data — and should not compete.
On dark backgrounds (Usiku, Baridi), Pumzi text only. Never grey text on dark — Ngozi is for dividers and secondary UI elements, not running text.
Syne headlines on photography: always add a subtle dark scrim (20–40% opacity) under the text unless the image is already dark enough. Legibility is a brand decision.
